behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the external speech-to-text API. A pure mock would always return correctly formatted speaker labels, hiding real API failures like speaker misdetection, malformed labels, or detection timeout. You must call the real API with real meeting audio to verify speaker labels are actually output.
behaviors.test_description: Test with real meeting audio containing 2+ speakers speaking sequentially. Call the real speech-to-text API endpoint. Verify the output includes speaker identifiers (e.g., 'Speaker1:', 'Speaker2:') correctly aligned with each transcript segment. Verify these labels are extracted and passed to the downstream summary step without corruption.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real API call but not multiple agentic steps or full pipeline coordination.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in LLM extraction quality, not in mock-able infrastructure. A mocked LLM would always extract action items correctly; the real LLM might miss implicit assignments ('someone will follow up'), misattribute actions to wrong attendees, or fail to parse indirect task language. Only real LLM calls expose these failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test with a realistic transcript containing multiple attendees explicitly and implicitly assigned tasks (e.g., 'Alice will investigate the error', 'Someone needs to write the post-mortem by Friday'). Call the real LLM with a structured prompt to extract action items. Verify the output includes at least one action item per assigned attendee. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real LLM call to catch extraction failures. Lower levels (mock LLM) would always pass.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the 'decisions' section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in how the real LLM structures and extracts decisions. A mock would always output properly structured decisions; the real LLM might confuse decisions with action items, place them in the narrative, or hallucinate non-existent decisions. You need real LLM calls to catch these failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test with a realistic transcript containing 3-5 explicit key decisions (e.g., 'We approved the Q2 budget', 'Decided to migrate to PostgreSQL', 'Agreed to partner with vendor X'). Call the real LLM to summarize with a structured output schema. Verify decisions are extracted into the 'decisions' field, not scattered in other sections. Verify decisions are distinct from action items and not duplicated across sections.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real LLM extraction to expose failures in decision identification and placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is in merging multiple LLM-generated summaries coherently while preserving speaker context. Mocks would merge without error. You need to execute the real sequence: split audio → call speech-to-text on each segment (real API) → call LLM to summarize each (real LLM) → call LLM again to merge (real LLM) → verify coherence. Context loss, speaker attribution misalignment, and information loss across segments only appear with real LLM calls.
behaviors.test_description: Test with real audio >60 minutes (or synthetic test recording simulating this). Verify segmentation logic splits at boundaries without losing audio. For each segment, call the real speech-to-text API and verify speaker labels are preserved. For each resulting transcript, call the real LLM to generate a segment summary. Then call the real LLM with all segment summaries to produce a final merged summary. Verify the final summary is coherent (no contradictions), speaker attributions are maintained across segments, and no information is lost in the merge.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multiple sequential LLM calls (summarize + merge) to catch real failures in coherence and context preservation across segments.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is the end-to-end agent pipeline. Real failures include upload timeouts, API rate-limiting, format incompatibility between steps, speech-to-text failure, LLM timeout, and integration breakdowns. Mocks hide all of these. You need the complete workflow with real APIs, real file handling, real network calls, and real LLM execution to expose failures in coordination, resilience, and error handling.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end test with a real >30 minute meeting recording (or a representative test file). Upload the audio file through the service. Trigger the full pipeline and monitor each step: transcription (real API), summarization (real LLM), structured output formatting. Verify the service completes without errors, crashes, timeouts, or requiring user intervention. Verify the structured output (action items, decisions, attendee attributions) is delivered and readable. Verify output files/APIs are accessible. Test on realistic network and API quota conditions to expose rate-limiting or timeout failures.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires the complete workflow with real infrastructure to catch failures in pipeline orchestration, error handling, and integration.
